name: Saad ibn Hareth Ansari
title: Awakened Old Man
shortDescription: An old man who came to Karbala with the Kufan army, but on Ashura afternoon, joined the Imam upon hearing his oppressed cry and was martyred.
fullStory:
Sa'd ibn al-Harith al-Ansari, along with his brother 'Abu al-Hutuf', were considered among the Kharijites of Kufa (or those influenced by Umayyad propaganda).

They came to Karbala as soldiers of Umar ibn Sa'd's army to fight Imam Hussain (AS). Throughout the day of Ashura, they witnessed the battles and the martyrdom of the Imam's companions while standing in the wrong army.

But on the afternoon of Ashura, after the martyrdom of all companions and Bani Hashem, the cry for help of Imam Hussain (AS) and the wailing of women and children suddenly rose from the tents. This oppressed plea awakened the sleeping conscience of these two old brothers.

Sa'd and his brother cried and said: 'We say "Judgment belongs only to God" and fear sins, but now we do not help the grandson of our Prophet?' They suddenly drew their swords and attacked Umar Sa'd's army. After killing several enemies, these two brothers attained martyrdom in the final moments of Ashura, achieving a blessed end.
quoteArabic: أَلا ناصِرٌ يَنْصُرُنا... أَلا ذابٌّ يَذُبُّ عَنْ حَرَمِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ...
quote: (The call that awakened them:) Is there any helper to help us? Is there any defender to defend the sanctuary of the Messenger of God?
